title: Xeraea scapigera
taxonomy: Xeraea scapigera belongs to the family Aizoaceae, commonly known as the succulent or viviparous plant family. It is a genus of flowering plants native to the Western Cape Province in South Africa. Xeraea scapigera is a type of succulent plant that stores water in its leaves, stems, or roots to survive in arid environments. The plant's classification is further divided into different species, each with unique characteristics and adaptations. The Aizoaceae family consists of over 1,800 species of succulents, including Xeraea scapigera.
